source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from ISO 7176-8 test-relevant sub-assemblies (side frames, cross members, seat rails, backrest uprights, armrest mounts, footrest brackets, caster forks, rear axle plates, push-rim handles, welded gussets) as required to realize the static/impact/fatigue load paths described in the original brief. Rulebook citations inherited verbatim from the original sample; no new citations added.

full_prompt:
Design one representative manual rigid-frame adult wheelchair frame (cross-tube or rigid-fold, depending on design choice) compliant with ISO 7176-8:2014 (static, impact, and fatigue strength) for a nominal occupant mass of 100 kg (ISO wheelchair test dummy). The frame must be a single welded or bolted tubular aluminum assembly (typical 6061-T6 aluminum, 25-32 mm OD seat tubes, 1.5-2.5 mm wall), supporting a 450 mm wide x 400 mm deep seat, 4 wheels (two 610 mm / 24 inch rear drive wheels + two 200 mm / 8 inch front casters), armrests, footrest(s), and a backrest, total assembly mass target <= 15 kg. The frame must pass three independent ISO 7176-8 evaluations: (A) STATIC: 1.25 times the nominal downward seat load (i.e. 1.25 * 100 kg * 9.81 m/s^2 = 1226 N) applied through the seat dummy test plate; 1.25 x nominal on backrest, armrest, and footrest per the clause-specific static pad locations; permanent deformation must not exceed the clause limits and the frame must not fracture. (B) IMPACT: 6666 equivalent curb-drop impacts from 50 mm height per the double-drum / curb-drop rig with the 100 kg dummy installed; no fracture or loss of function (ISO 7176-8 curb-drop cycle count). (C) FATIGUE: 200,000 cycles on the two-drum fatigue machine with 100 kg dummy at the prescribed drum geometry; no fracture or loss of function. Target material is 6061-T6 aluminum alloy (yield 276 MPa, UTS 310 MPa, E = 68.9 GPa, density 2700 kg/m3). Deliverable is a single CAD assembly of the frame (tubes + welded gussets + wheel/axle mounts + caster forks + seat/back + armrest + footrest) suitable for linear-static structural FEA under ISO 7176-8 clause-specific static loads, explicit-dynamics curb-drop impact, and fatigue life evaluation to 200000 + 6666 cycles. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling analyses are NOT required. Explicit-dynamics impact solver and fatigue solver are mandatory non-FEA-static solvers.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
This design is delivered as a multi-part welded/bolted tubular assembly rather than a single solid body. The decomposition follows the load-path architecture demanded by ISO 7176-8 static, curb-drop impact, and two-drum fatigue tests: two main side frame tubes (P1) carry seat and occupant load to the rear axle plates (P8) and caster forks (P7); two transverse cross members (P2) stiffen the frame laterally and provide the curb-drop impact load path; two seat pan rails (P3) distribute seat-dummy 1226 N load into the side frames; two backrest uprights (P4) tie into the rear of the side frames and react the backrest clause static load; two armrest mounts (P5) and two footrest brackets (P6) react the armrest/footrest 1.25x static pads; two caster forks (P7) and two rear axle plates (P8) transmit wheel reactions and curb-drop impact; two push-rim attachment bosses (P9) on the rear axle plates transfer hand-rim load; welded gussets (P10) reinforce the four high-moment tube-to-tube joints where curb-drop impact and fatigue stress concentrate; and a fastener family (P11) of M6-M8 stainless bolts secures bolted joints (quick-release wheels, armrests, footrests). The final FEM verification runs on the ASSEMBLED model only - per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Inter-part connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) at the welded gusset-to-tube joints and at bolted armrest/footrest/wheel interfaces must be <= 1.0 under the governing ISO 7176-8 static and curb-drop cases. All inherited load magnitudes (1226 N seat, 50 mm drop, 6666 curb-drop cycles, 200000 two-drum cycles), material properties (6061-T6 Al, 276 MPa yield), and pass/fail thresholds from the original single-part brief are preserved verbatim; assembly-scope criteria are added.
short_prompt:
Design a manual wheelchair frame as a multi-part welded-tube assembly per ISO 7176-8:2014 static, impact, and fatigue tests. Occupant mass 100 kg; total frame mass <= 15 kg; frame tubing Al 6061-T6 yield 276 MPa.

Load cases: LC1 static load 1226 N at the seat per ISO 7176-8; LC2 50 mm drop impact; LC3 fatigue 200000 cycles at the main frame joints and 6666 cycles at the caster fork.

Pass/fail: no yield under LC1/LC2; fatigue life satisfies LC3 cycle counts; FEM at assembled level; weld and pivot DCR <= 1.0.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Curb-drop 50 mm impact from 6666 drops requires transient dynamics with large-displacement contact (not linear static FEA).
200000-cycle double-drum fatigue cannot be evaluated with a single static FEA run.

prompt.title: Adult manual wheelchair frame qualified to ISO 7176-8 (multi-part welded tubular assembly)
prompt.brief: Design a rigid manual wheelchair frame for a 100 kg ISO test dummy passing static (1.25 x nominal), impact (6666 x 50 mm drops), and fatigue (200000 double-drum cycles) per ISO 7176-8. [Multi-part assembly] side frame tubes (x2), cross members (x2), seat pan rails (x2), backrest uprights (x2), armrest mounts (x2), footrest brackets (x2), caster forks (x2), rear axle plates (x2), push-rim bosses (x2), welded gussets (x4), fastener family (x16). FEM verification at assembled level only; welded and bolted joint DCR <= 1.0 under governing static + curb-drop cases.

boundary_conditions.wheel_contacts: rear wheel and caster contact points provide vertical + tangential reaction
boundary_conditions.static_tests: hard floor under wheels; loads applied via ISO 7176-8 test pads

parts.name: main side frame tube
parts.role: left/right longitudinal spine carrying seat, backrest, and rear-axle loads to caster forks and rear axle plates; primary bending member under LC1 and curb-drop LC4

parts.name: transverse cross member
parts.role: lateral stiffener between left/right side frames; carries curb-drop torsional reaction and maintains 450 mm seat-width alignment under LC4

parts.name: seat pan rail
parts.role: left/right under-seat rail that distributes the 1226 N ISO seat-pad static load (LC1) into the side frame tubes

parts.name: backrest upright
parts.role: left/right rear upright rising from side frame tubes to backrest height; reacts ISO 7176-8 backrest static pad load (LC2)

parts.name: armrest mount bracket
parts.role: left/right bolted bracket clamping to backrest upright and side frame, carrying armrest 1.25x static pad load (LC3)

parts.name: footrest bracket
parts.role: left/right forward bracket welded/bolted to front of side frame; carries footrest 1.25x static pad load (LC3)

geometric_constraints.form: welded L-bracket with cross-hole for footrest tube

parts.name: caster fork
parts.role: front wheel fork holding 200 mm caster; transmits front-wheel reaction and absorbs 50 mm curb-drop impact (LC4)

parts.name: rear axle plate
parts.role: left/right machined plate welded to rear of side frame; mounts 12 mm quick-release axle of 610 mm rear drive wheel; governs LC4 curb-drop and LC5 two-drum fatigue load path

parts.name: push-rim attachment boss
parts.role: threaded boss on rear axle plate carrying push-rim standoff; transmits hand-rim propulsion load into axle plate

parts.name: welded gusset
parts.role: triangular reinforcement at high-moment tube-to-tube joints (side-frame-to-backrest-upright, side-frame-to-cross-member); critical for curb-drop and two-drum fatigue life

parts.name: fastener family
parts.role: M6-M8 stainless socket-head cap screws and nylock nuts for bolted interfaces (armrest, footrest, quick-release rear axle retention plate, push-rim standoff)

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: origin at ground-projection of rear axle centerline midpoint; +X forward, +Z up

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: yield of 6061-T6; no permanent deformation beyond ISO 7176-8 clause limits

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection demand-to-capacity ratio (DCR) across all welded gusset joints (IF1, IF5) and bolted interfaces (IF2, IF3, IF4, IF6) <= 1.0 under the governing ISO 7176-8 static (LC1-LC3) and curb-drop (LC4) cases.
Assembled-frame static deflection at the seat-pad center under LC1 (1226 N) shall not exceed 10 mm, ensuring the ISO 7176-8 permanent-deformation clause is not violated after load removal.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: beam + shell for tubes, solid for welded gussets, rigid dummy

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded tube-to-tube joint with gusset P10 reinforcement
interfaces.load_path: Cross member P2 transfers curb-drop lateral and torsional reaction into side frame tubes P1; gusset P10 distributes moment concentration

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded saddle joint (seat rail to side frame)
interfaces.load_path: Seat pan rail P3 funnels LC1 seat 1226 N load into side frame tube P1 via welded saddle

interfaces.mediated_by: TIG-welded lap joint with gusset P10
interfaces.load_path: Backrest upright P4 transfers LC2 backrest static load into rear of side frame tube P1

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted (2x M6) via fastener family P11
interfaces.load_path: Armrest mount P5 transfers LC3 armrest 1.25x static load into backrest upright P4 through M6 bolted joint
Footrest bracket P6 transfers LC3 footrest 1.25x static load into front of side frame tube P1
Caster fork P7 transmits front-wheel vertical reaction and curb-drop impact (LC4) into front of side frame tube P1
Rear axle plate P8 transmits 610 mm drive-wheel reaction, curb-drop impact (LC4), and two-drum fatigue cycles (LC5) into rear of side frame tube P1
Push-rim attachment boss P9 transmits hand-rim propulsion load into rear axle plate P8

interfaces.mediated_by: quick-release axle retention via fastener family P11
interfaces.load_path: Quick-release axle retained by rear axle plate P8 with M8 hardware from fastener family P11

fem_check_policy.rationale: ISO 7176-8 static, curb-drop impact, and two-drum fatigue tests are defined on the complete wheelchair assembly on a test floor with the ISO test dummy; per-part stress is reported for diagnostic purposes but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A manual wheelchair frame is inherently a welded/bolted tubular assembly of >15 load-bearing members (side frames, cross members, seat rails, uprights, brackets, forks, axle plates, gussets, fasteners). Single-solid representation would misstate welded-joint stress concentration and hide the curb-drop/fatigue-critical gusseted interfaces.
notes.speculation_caveats: parts[] counts (e.g., 4 welded gussets, 16 fasteners) and specific tube ODs/wall thicknesses are speculation within the 25-32 mm OD / 1.5-2.5 mm wall range stated by the original brief; ISO 7176-8 does not prescribe member count.
